entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., the target crop of the research

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A widespread and destructive disease of pigeonpea caused by Fusarium udum Butler

entities.name: FUSARIUM UDUM BUTLER
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes Fusarium wilt in pigeonpea

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where pigeonpea and Fusarium wilt are prevalent

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where pigeonpea and Fusarium wilt are prevalent

entities.name: AMERICAS
entities.description: A region where pigeonpea diseases were surveyed

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A major pigeonpea-producing country with high wilt incidence

entities.name: MAHARASHTRA
entities.description: An Indian state with a reported 23% wilt incidence

entities.name: BIHAR
entities.description: An Indian state with a reported 18% wilt incidence

entities.name: UTTAR PRADESH
entities.description: An Indian state with a reported 15% wilt incidence

entities.name: MALAWI
entities.description: An African country where wilt is the only major pigeonpea disease

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: An African country where wilt is the only major pigeonpea disease

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: An African country where wilt is the only major pigeonpea disease

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The agricultural productivity of pigeonpea, which is reduced by Fusarium wilt

entities.name: WILT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pigeonpea to resist infection by Fusarium wilt

entities.name: IDENTIFYING RESISTANT LINES
entities.description: The process of finding pigeonpea lines that exhibit resistance to wilt

entities.name: MULTILOCATIONAL SCREENING
entities.description: The process of testing pigeonpea lines for resistance across multiple locations

entities.name: DEVELOPING RESISTANT CULTIVARS
entities.description: The process of creating pigeonpea varieties that are resistant to wilt

entities.name: GENETIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: Resistance to Fusarium wilt based on the genetic makeup of the plant

entities.name: CULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: Non-genetic agricultural methods used to control disease

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: A cultural practice involving rotating crops to control Fusarium wilt

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: A cultural practice involving mixed or intercropping to control Fusarium wilt
